content:
type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) to outlines/prompts.py

fields.summary: Adding global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) to outlines/prompts.py

content:
type: STATUS
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding register_filter()/unregister_filter() with a module-level _FILTERS registry dict; will merge it into env.filters in _template_from_str/_template_from_file right after the built-in filters are set (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), before any local filters you add, so that local filters you pass in create_jinja_env can override registered ones by applying them last.

fields.summary: Adding register_filter()/unregister_filter() with a module-level _FILTERS registry dict; will merge it into env.filters in _template_from_str/_template_from_file right after the built-in filters are set (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), before any local filters you add, so that local filters you pass in create_jinja_env can override registered ones by applying them last.

content:
type: ANSWER
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Confirmed compatible: module-level _FILTERS dict populated by register_filter(name,func)/unregister_filter(name); in _template_from_str and _template_from_file I call env.filters.update(_FILTERS) immediately after the 6 built-in filters are set, before any local filters. Matches your base->registry->per-call order as long as create_jinja_env applies its filters arg last.

fields.summary: Confirmed compatible: module-level _FILTERS dict populated by register_filter(name,func)/unregister_filter(name); in _template_from_str and _template_from_file I call env.filters.update(_FILTERS) immediately after the 6 built-in filters are set, before any local filters.
